description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof clad in red shingles. The structure features natural wood siding and exposed timber posts supporting a covered porch area on one side. The front facade includes a set of double doors with large glass panels and a single rectangular window, while the rear and one side are windowless, emphasizing privacy. The side opposite the porch has a large, barn-style double door with visible metal hinges, suggesting a possible garage or storage function. The roof has a moderate overhang, providing some shelter around the perimeter. The overall design is simple yet functional, blending rustic charm with practical urban utility.
description: A wooden cabin with a red gable roof, covered porch, and both glass and barn-style doors. The design is rustic and functional, with natural wood siding and minimal windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, double glass doors, and a single window on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large barn-style double door and the gable end of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with a solid wooden wall and no visible openings.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in red shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: one medium-sized rectangular window on the front left, asymmetrically placed; no other windows visible
attribute_door_placement: double glass doors centered on the front facade under the porch, large barn-style double door on the right-side gable end
